text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
SURVEILLANCE STUDY

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Ms. Marisol Fuentes-Delgado is a 59-year-old Hispanic woman with a history of left breast triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma (T2N1M0, stage IIB at diagnosis; ypT1cN1mi post-neoadjuvant, RCB-II), germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC). She underwent neoadjuvant chemotherapy with dose-dense doxo/cyclo followed by weekly pacli, concurrent with Keytruda, followed by left modified radical mastectomy with contralateral prophylactic right mastectomy and sentinel lymph node biopsy (1 of 3 SLNs positive for micrometastasis, 2.1 mm). She subsequently completed post-mastectomy radiation therapy (50.4 Gy to left chest wall and regional nodes with 10 Gy boost; completed approximately 6 weeks post-surgery). Radiation was complicated by grade 2 radiation pneumonitis, treated with oral prednisone taper (now resolved). She is currently receiving adjuvant cape (dose-reduced to 750 mg/m2 BID, days 1-14, q21-day cycle) and pembro 200 mg IV q3 weeks; currently on cycle 4. This examination is performed for routine surveillance of treatment response and to evaluate for distant recurrence. Comparison: Prior surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed approximately 10 months ago (no evidence of disease at that time). Also compared against CT performed at staging (prior to neoadjuvant therapy).

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed with intravenous nonionic contrast material administered via antecubital vein. Axial images were acquired in the portal venous phase. Multiplanar reformatted images in coronal and sagittal planes were reviewed. Oral contrast was not administered. Radiation dose modulation was employed.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. The lungs are fully expanded. There are no new pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidative opacities. Specifically, the previously documented areas of ground-glass opacity in the left lower and mid-lung zones — which were present on prior imaging and attributed to radiation pneumonitis — have resolved completely. No residual ground-glass attenuation, no organizing pneumonia, and no fibrotic changes are identified in the expected radiation field. The right lung is clear throughout. No pleural effusions. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The heart is mildly enlarged in transverse dimension, unchanged compared to prior studies. No pericardial effusion. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of aneurysm or dissection. The mediastinum is otherwise unremarkable. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes are identified. Specifically, the prevascular, paratracheal, subcarinal, and paraesophageal nodal stations show no nodes meeting size criteria for pathologic enlargement. The bilateral hilar regions are symmetric and unremarkable without adenopathy.

Left Chest Wall and Mastectomy Site:
Postoperative changes are noted in the left chest wall consistent with left modified radical mastectomy. There is expected skin thickening and subcutaneous architectural distortion in the left anterior chest wall. Tissue expander is present in the left mastectomy bed with expected configuration and no surrounding fluid collection, hematoma, or abscess. Radiation-related soft tissue changes are noted along the left anterior and lateral chest wall, with mild skin thickening in the radiation field — these findings are stable compared to prior imaging and consistent with post-radiation sequelae. No discrete enhancing soft tissue mass or nodule is identified at or near the prior tumor bed to suggest locoregional recurrence. No chest wall invasion.

Right Chest Wall and Prophylactic Mastectomy Site:
Postoperative changes are noted in the right chest wall consistent with right total mastectomy. Tissue expander is present in the right mastectomy bed with expected configuration. No peri-expander fluid collection or concerning soft tissue nodularity.

Axillary and Supraclavicular Regions:
No pathologically enlarged left axillary lymph nodes are identified. The previously documented enlarged left axillary lymph node (which underwent FNA biopsy at diagnosis) is no longer identified; residual post-treatment architectural change is present without discrete nodal enlargement. No right axillary adenopathy. No supraclavicular or infraclavicular lymphadenopathy bilaterally. Left internal mammary nodal chain: no adenopathy.

Bones of the Thorax:
No destructive osseous lesions are identified in the visualized ribs, sternum, clavicles, or thoracic vertebral bodies. No lytic or sclerotic foci to suggest metastatic involvement. Mild degenerative changes are present along the thoracic spine, most prominent at T7-T8 and T10-T11 with small anterior osteophytes, consistent with spondylosis deformans. No vertebral height loss or cortical destruction.

Soft Tissues of the Chest:
No abnormal soft tissue masses. Port-a-cath or mediport access device is noted in the right subclavian/superior vena cava region; catheter tip is appropriately positioned at the cavoatrial junction without evidence of thrombosis or infection.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size and contour. The hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement in the portal venous phase without focal hypoattenuating or hyperattenuating lesions. Specifically, no discrete hepatic nodules or masses are identified in any hepatic segment. The hepatic veins and portal vein are patent. The intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated. No perihepatic fluid.

Gallbladder and Biliary System:
The gallbladder is present without wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid, or intraluminal filling defects. The common bile duct is not dilated, measuring approximately 4 mm in maximal diameter.

Spleen:
The spleen is normal in size, shape, and attenuation. No focal splenic lesions. No perisplenic fluid.

Pancreas:
The pancreas demonstrates homogeneous enhancement throughout. The pancreatic duct is not dilated. No focal pancreatic masses or ductal abnormalities. The peripancreatic fat planes are preserved.

Adrenal Glands:
Bilateral adrenal glands are unremarkable in size and morphology. No adrenal nodules or masses. No adrenal thickening.

Kidneys and Ureters:
The kidneys are symmetric in size and enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No renal calculi within the limits of this non-dedicated study. No perirenal or perinephric collections. Mild cortical thinning bilaterally is present, consistent with chronic nephropathy in the setting of longstanding hypertension and diabetes mellitus — stable compared to prior examination. No new cortical abnormality or perinephric stranding to suggest acute process.

Bowel:
The stomach, small bowel, and large bowel loops are unremarkable in caliber and wall thickness. No bowel obstruction. No free intraperitoneal air. The appendix is visualized and appears unremarkable.

Abdominal Aorta and Vasculature:
The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber without aneurysm or significant atherosclerotic disease. The celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and inferior mesenteric artery origins appear patent. No retroperitoneal hematoma.

Abdominal Lymph Nodes:
No retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or para-aortic lymph nodes meeting size criteria for pathologic enlargement. No suspicious nodal masses.

Ascites:
No free peritoneal fluid.

---

PELVIS:

Gynecologic Structures:
The patient is postmenopausal and has undergone bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (noted on prior records related to BRCA1 mutation management — no uterus or ovaries are identified). Postsurgical changes are present in the pelvis without concerning soft tissue mass or pelvic lymphadenopathy. The vaginal cuff, if present, is not clearly delineated on this study but no suspicious pelvic soft tissue is seen.

Bladder:
The urinary bladder is partially distended and appears normal in wall thickness. No intraluminal filling defect.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
No enlarged inguinal, iliac, or obturator lymph nodes identified. No pelvic sidewall adenopathy.

Pelvic Bones:
No destructive osseous lesions are identified in the visualized pelvis including the iliac wings, sacrum, acetabula, or proximal femora. Mild degenerative changes of the sacroiliac joints and hip joints, consistent with patient's known osteoarthritis, unchanged compared to prior.

Soft Tissues:
No significant subcutaneous or deep pelvic soft tissue abnormality. No pelvic free fluid.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of locoregional recurrence or distant metastatic disease on this surveillance examination. The imaging findings are reassuring in the context of ongoing adjuvant therapy.

2. Complete resolution of the previously identified ground-glass opacities in the left lung attributed to radiation pneumonitis. No residual inflammatory or fibrotic changes.

3. Expected postoperative changes at bilateral mastectomy sites with bilateral tissue expanders in place, without concerning periprosthetic collections, soft tissue masses, or nodular enhancement to suggest local recurrence at either mastectomy bed.

4. Post-radiation changes in the left anterior chest wall are stable and consistent with expected sequelae of prior post-mastectomy radiotherapy.

5. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal, axillary, hilar, abdominal, or pelvic lymph nodes.

6. Normal-appearing liver, spleen, adrenal glands, and pancreas without lesions. No hepatic metastases.

7. Stable bilateral cortical thinning of the kidneys, consistent with chronic nephropathy in the setting of longstanding hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus.

8. No suspicious osseous lesions in the visualized axial or appendicular skeleton. No lytic or sclerotic metastatic foci identified.

Overall, this surveillance study demonstrates no imaging evidence of disease recurrence or new metastatic involvement. Findings are consistent with a favorable treatment response during adjuvant chemotherapy and immunotherapy.
